normalized_title: State Planning Policy No. 3.4 Natural Hazards and Disasters
version_date: Gazetted 11 April 2006 (Special Gazette No. 67; policy dated April 2006)
scope_summary: State Planning Policy made under section 26 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 requiring natural hazards (flood, cyclone, severe storm, storm surge, tsunami, coastal erosion, bushfire, landslide, earthquake) to be considered as a fundamental element in planning. It applies throughout Western Australia to regional and local strategies, schemes and amendments, structure plans, subdivision, strata subdivision and development applications. Local governments must have regard to it when preparing or amending schemes, strategies and policies and when advising on applications on hazard-affected land.

key_numeric_standards.topic: defined flood event for floodplain development control
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: development on floodplains; habitable, commercial and industrial buildings to have floor levels above the defined flood event level
development on steep slopes; may trigger additional geotechnical investigation
